blockingIssues.description: `crates/vera-core/src/storage/metadata.rs::parse_language()` does not map `csharp`, `php`, `haskell`, `elixir`, or `dart`, so persisted chunks for those languages deserialize as `Language::Unknown` and break language filters/result metadata.

`crates/vera-core/src/parsing/extractor.rs` no longer contains a dedicated `scala_extracts_types_and_functions` test, regressing previously delivered Scala coverage and leaving milestone-wide extractor coverage incomplete.

`classify_hcl()` maps every HCL/Terraform block to `SymbolType::Struct`, so `resource`, `data`, `variable`, `output`, and `module` are not distinguished as required by the feature description and VAL-LANG-015.
`README.md` still summarizes language support as 'Rust, Python, TypeScript, Go, Java, C/C++, and more' instead of listing the full 21+ supported languages required by VAL-LANG-019.
`SKILL.md` says Vera parses '21+ languages with tree-sitter' but does not enumerate the supported languages, so VAL-LANG-019 is still unmet.

appliedUpdates.description: Documented the required `storage/metadata.rs::parse_language()` round-trip mapping when adding new `Language` variants, because forward enum/extension wiring alone is insufficient for indexed chunks read back from SQLite.

Documented the tree-sitter grammar compatibility workaround (git-sourced HCL binding, vendored SQL/Protobuf parsers compiled in `build.rs`) and clarified that nested member extraction remains language-dependent unless a language-specific helper exists.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Extend the tree-sitter language support checklist to include `crates/vera-core/src/storage/metadata.rs::parse_language()` whenever a new `Language` variant or `Language::as_str()` value is introduced.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The `languages-new-popular` review found that C#, PHP, Haskell, Elixir, and Dart were added in `types.rs` and extractor dispatch but omitted from `parse_language()`, causing persisted chunks to deserialize as `Language::Unknown`.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Align `implementation-worker` with enforceable expectations: either require workers to explicitly record deviations when they skip ADR review, red-phase tests, or manual CLI verification, or relax the procedure so handoffs do not claim `followedProcedure: true` when those steps were omitted.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Both `languages-new-popular` and `languages-config-infra` reported `followedProcedure: true`, but their transcript skeletons did not show the skill's required ADR review, failing-test-first step, or manual `vera index/search` verification.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: For parser/extractor features, require tests to assert expected symbol names/types (not just non-empty output) before a feature can be considered validated.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The `languages-config-infra` feature added HCL/SQL/Protobuf smoke tests that only asserted non-empty extraction output, which allowed the missing HCL block typing and incomplete language-list documentation requirements to slip through implementation review until scrutiny.

rejectedObservations.observation: Feature wording that asks for '15 non-fallthrough arms' in `classify_node()` is ambiguous because TypeScript and JavaScript intentionally share one match arm.
rejectedObservations.reason: mission-specific wording ambiguity; no clear durable shared-state target beyond this milestone's feature text
